prompt: NEC Article 210 covers:

options.text: Branch circuits and outlets
Swimming pool chemistry exclusively always always
HVAC refrigerant types exclusively always always
Plumbing vent sizing exclusively always always

explanation: Article 210 establishes branch-circuit ratings and requirements.

prompt: The NEC is published by:

options.text: FDA exclusively always always
NFPA (National Fire Protection Association)
USDA exclusively always always
FAA exclusively always always

explanation: NFPA 70 is the National Electrical Code.

prompt: A continuous load runs for:

options.text: Exactly 30 seconds always always
Less than 1 minute always always defining continuous always always
3 hours or more
Only during lightning storms always always

explanation: NEC defines continuous load as expected 3 hours or more.

prompt: Equipment grounding conductors (EGC) provide:

options.text: Normal return path for load current always always exclusively always
Only lightning protection exclusively always always with no fault role ever always always
Decorative conduit support exclusively always always
Fault current path to clear overcurrent devices quickly

explanation: EGC bonds equipment to ground for safety during faults.

distractorExplanations.a: Neutral carries normal current in some systems.

prompt: Article 250 addresses:

options.text: Grounding and bonding
Kitchen appliance recipes exclusively always always
Roof shingle colors exclusively always always
Window tint exclusively always always

explanation: Article 250 is central grounding/bonding requirements.

prompt: Working space about electrical equipment (110.26) requires:

options.text: Storage of combustibles in front of panels always always
Clear depth, width, and height for safe operation and maintenance
Zero clearance always always acceptable always always
Only 6 inches width always always regardless of equipment always always

explanation: 110.26 specifies minimum clear working space by voltage condition.

distractorExplanations.d: Width at least 30 inches or equipment width.

prompt: GFCI protection is required for 125V, 15A and 20A receptacles in:

options.text: Only industrial 480V motor starters always always exclusively always
No locations ever always under any NEC edition always always
Bathrooms, garages, outdoors, and other locations per 210.8
Only data centers always always exclusively always

explanation: 210.8 lists dwelling and other locations requiring GFCI.

prompt: AFCI protection in dwelling units protects:

options.text: Only ground faults exclusively always as AFCI function always always
Only lightning exclusively always always
Only overloads exclusively always always without arc detection ever always always
Branch circuits from arc faults that can cause fires

explanation: AFCI devices detect dangerous arcing on circuits.

prompt: Box fill calculations (314.16) ensure:

options.text: Conductor volume in box does not exceed box capacity
Unlimited wires in any box always always
Only box color matters always always
No splices allowed ever always in boxes always always

explanation: Each conductor/stripe/device counts toward cubic inch allowance.

prompt: The ampacity of conductors is found in:

options.text: Only guesswork always always without tables ever always always
Table 310.16 (or applicable tables) with adjustments
Plumbing code tables exclusively always always
Food calorie charts exclusively always always

explanation: 310.16 lists ampacities; 310.15 adjustments for temp/bundling.

prompt: Dedicated equipment space (110.26(E)) above panelboards:

options.text: May be filled with ductwork always always without restriction always always
Only applies to battery clocks always always
Must be kept clear of foreign systems to 6 ft above or structural ceiling
Is optional always always in all occupancies always always

explanation: Foreign systems cannot encroach dedicated electrical space.

prompt: Receptacle spacing in dwelling unit walls (210.52) generally requires:

options.text: One receptacle per entire house always always
Receptacles only in attic always always
No receptacles in kitchens ever always
Receptacles so no point along floor line is more than 6 ft from a receptacle

explanation: 210.52(A) 6-foot rule along wall lines; specific rooms have additional.

prompt: Overcurrent protection must not exceed conductor ampacity except:

options.text: Where 240.4(B) or specific exceptions allow next standard size up
Never any exception ever always under NEC always always
Always double ampacity always always as rule always always
Only for plumbing pipes always always

explanation: 240.4 governs OCPD sizing relative to conductor ampacity.

prompt: Listed equipment means:

options.text: Any homemade device without evaluation always always acceptable always always
Evaluated by recognized testing lab for safety standard compliance
Only listed in phone book always always
Approved by neighbor always always

explanation: Listing (UL etc.) indicates tested to applicable standards.

prompt: NEC scope (Article 90) covers:

options.text: Only utility transmission beyond service point always always exclusively always
Only battery manufacturing always always exclusively always
Installation of electrical conductors and equipment
Only solar manufacturing plants always always exclusively always without installation always always

explanation: NEC applies to premises wiring from service to utilization.

prompt: NM-B (Romex) cable is permitted in:

options.text: Underground direct burial always always without conduit ever always always
Commercial parking garage exposed always always always
Swimming pool interior always always always
Dry, protected locations per 334 (typically not wet or exposed)

explanation: 334 restricts NM use; not in wet/damp or subject to physical damage.

prompt: Conductor color: grounded (neutral) conductor is typically:

options.text: White or gray
Green always always for neutral always always
Bare only always always for insulated neutral always always
Red always always for all neutrals always always

explanation: 200.6 identifies white/gray for grounded conductor.

prompt: Equipment grounding conductor insulation color is typically:

options.text: White always always for EGC always always
Green or bare
Blue exclusively always always for all EGC always always
Orange exclusively always always for all EGC always always

explanation: 250.119 specifies green or bare for EGC.

prompt: Maximum number of 12 AWG THHN conductors in 1/2" EMT depends on:

options.text: Always unlimited always always in any conduit always always
Only conduit color always always
Chapter 9 Table 1 and conductor fill (often about 9 for 40% fill)
Only voltage always always without fill calculation ever always always

explanation: Annex C tables give conductor fill limits per raceway size.

prompt: Splicing conductors must be made in:

options.text: Hidden inside wall without access always always always
Only with tape and no box always always as permanent always always
Under carpet always always without enclosure always always
Approved box or fitting with accessible cover

explanation: 300.15 requires box for splices unless exempt equipment.

prompt: Voltage drop should be considered because excessive drop causes:

options.text: Poor equipment performance and wasted energy
Improved motor torque always always from low voltage always always
No effect ever always on loads always always
Automatic code compliance always always regardless of length always always

explanation: 210.19(A) informational note suggests ~3% branch/feeder limits often used.

prompt: Three-way switches control a load from:

options.text: Only one location always always exclusively always
Two locations using traveler conductors between switches
Three locations without 4-way switches ever always always
Only with wireless magic always always without conductors ever always always

explanation: Two 3-way switches and travelers allow dual control.

prompt: MC cable differs from AC cable because MC:

options.text: Has no metal armor ever always always
Is only for 600V DC always always exclusively always
Uses full size aluminum interlocked armor as EGC (typical) without separate thin strip
Cannot be used indoors ever always always

explanation: AC has internal bonding strip; MC armor often serves as EGC.

prompt: Pull boxes are sized per:

options.text: Smallest box available always always regardless of pull always always
Only aesthetic preference always always
Random choice always always
314.28 based on conduit trade size and number of bends/conductors

explanation: 314.28 requires adequate space for conductor bending radius.

prompt: Identifying ungrounded conductors: 208Y/120V system typically uses:

options.text: Black, red, blue for phases; white neutral
All white always always for every conductor always always
Pink exclusively always always for all phases always always
No identification required ever always always

explanation: Common color code practice per 200.7 and local convention.

prompt: Switch loops must:

options.text: Never have neutral at switch always always in new work always always post-2011 NEC changes always always
Provide grounded conductor to switch location per current NEC dwelling requirements
Use only ungrounded conductors always always without exception ever always always in all cases always always
Bypass all boxes always always

explanation: 404.2(C) requires neutral at switch controlling loads in dwelling.

distractorExplanations.a: Neutral now required in many switch locations.

prompt: Underground service lateral conductors entering building must:

options.text: Lie on lawn surface always always without cover always always
Use extension cords always always as permanent always always
Be protected from physical damage and meet burial depth/raceway rules
Cross pool bottom unprotected always always

explanation: 300.5 specifies burial depths and raceway where required.

prompt: Multiwire branch circuits share:

options.text: Only one ungrounded conductor always always without neutral ever always always
No common neutral ever always always in any circuit always always
Only DC power always always exclusively always
Neutral with opposing ungrounded conductors on different phases

explanation: Multiwire circuits reduce neutral current when loads balanced.

prompt: Torque requirements for terminations:

options.text: Should follow manufacturer specifications; 110.14 requires proper tightening
Hand tight only always always for all lugs always always
Maximum impact wrench always always without spec always always
Never inspect after tightening always always

explanation: 110.14(A) and listing require adequate torque for reliable connection.

prompt: Cable support for NM-B must occur within:

options.text: 10 feet from every box always always exclusively always
12 inches of box and every 4.5 ft along run (334.30)
Never supported always always
Only at panel always always one support total always always

explanation: 334.30 requires stapling/support intervals.

prompt: Service disconnecting means must be:

options.text: Hidden behind locked mystery door always always without access always always
Only operable by utility always always with no customer disconnect ever always always
Readily accessible and located per 230.70 near meter/service entrance
Optional for all services always always under NEC always always

explanation: 230.70 requires main disconnect at readily accessible location.

prompt: Service entrance conductors are sized from:

options.text: Only breaker handle size always always without calculation ever always always
Wire color preference always always
Neighbor's service size always always
Calculated load per Article 220 and 230.42/230.82 rules

explanation: 220 load calc determines minimum service conductor ampacity.

prompt: Grounding electrode system for service includes:

options.text: Rod(s), plate, concrete encased electrode, or metal underground pipe where available
Only plastic pipe always always as electrode always always
Painted drywall screw always always as sole electrode always always
No grounding ever always required always always

explanation: 250.52 lists acceptable grounding electrodes.

prompt: Overhead service clearance from windows:

options.text: May touch window always always
Must meet 230.24(B) clearances from openings not designed for conductors
No clearances ever always specified always always
Only underground rules apply always always

explanation: 230.24 specifies vertical/horizontal clearances from buildings.

prompt: Meter socket is typically:

options.text: Same as main panel always always with all branch breakers always always inside meter always always
Only for gas metering always always
Utility/customer demarcation point; not load center unless combined
Optional decoration always always

explanation: Meter measures service; disconnect/panel often separate.

prompt: Service lateral vs service drop:

options.text: Identical terms always always interchangeable always always without difference ever always always
Lateral always overhead always always
Drop always underground always always
Lateral is underground; drop is overhead from utility

explanation: NEC definitions distinguish overhead drop vs underground lateral.

prompt: Parallel service conductors must:

options.text: Be same length, material, size, and termination per 310.10(H)
Different sizes always always in same phase always always
Share neutral only on one pipe always always unbalanced always always
Never need equal impedance always always

explanation: Parallel runs require equal current sharing characteristics.

prompt: 230.82 lists permitted loads connected to service conductors:

options.text: Every branch circuit directly always always without disconnect ever always always
Supply-side connections like certain solar, MGTs per rules—not all branch loads
Only lighting always always exclusively always
Nothing ever always connected always always

explanation: Most loads connect downstream of service disconnect; limited exceptions.

prompt: Neutral conductor on service carries:

options.text: Never any current always always under balanced load always always exclusively always
Only lightning always always
Unbalanced current and harmonic return on single-phase and some 3-phase
Only DC always always on AC service always always

explanation: Grounded conductor carries difference in phase currents.

prompt: Service equipment rating must be at least:

options.text: 15 amperes for all 400A homes always always
1 amp always always maximum always always
Unlimited without calculation always always
Calculated load but not less than minimums in 230.79 for dwelling

explanation: 230.79: dwelling minimum often 100A (3-wire) with exceptions.

prompt: Utility service point demarcation determines:

options.text: Who owns conductors and which code/rules apply beyond point
Only paint color always always
Only internet speed always always
Employee lunch menu always always

explanation: Ownership split between utility and customer wiring.

prompt: Transformers supplying building separately derived systems require:

options.text: No grounding ever always always
Bonding and grounding per 250.30
Only primary protection always always without secondary always always
Floating ungrounded always always without exception ever always always as mandatory always always

explanation: SDS systems need grounding electrode and bonding jumper rules.

prompt: Class 160 service overcurrent device maximum for dwelling:

options.text: 15A always always maximum service always always for all dwellings always always
10,000A minimum always always residential always always
Not standard—services typically 100A-400A; 160A may exist as equipment rating context
No OCPD allowed always always

explanation: Service OCPD sized to conductor and load; common 100-200A residential.

prompt: Emergency disconnect for one- and two-family dwellings (230.85):

options.text: Prohibits any disconnect always always
Only inside locked attic always always
Only for commercial always always never dwellings always always
Requires readily accessible external disconnect as of recent NEC cycles

explanation: 2020 NEC 230.85 added emergency disconnect requirement for dwellings.

prompt: Service raceway seal where underground conductors enter building:

options.text: May be required to prevent gas/moisture migration per 300.5(G) and 230.8
Never sealed always always
Filled with gasoline always always
Open hole preferred always always

explanation: Sealing prevents hazardous gas entry from underground ducts.

prompt: Arc flash hazard analysis helps determine:

options.text: Only wire color always always
PPE category and approach boundaries for energized work
Lunch break timing always always
Conduit paint shade always always

explanation: NFPA 70E uses incident energy to select PPE.

prompt: Lockout/tagout before electrical work:

options.text: Work hot always always as first choice always always
Only tag without locking always always sufficient always always alone always always
De-energize and verify absence of voltage before contact
Ask coworker to guess always always if live always always

explanation: 110.13/OSHA require verified de-energization for most work.

distractorExplanations.a: Energized work only where permitted with PPE.

prompt: Insulated tools are rated by:

options.text: Weight only always always
Handle color only always always without rating ever always always
Price exclusively always always
ASTM/ISO standards for voltage rating (e.g., 1000V)

explanation: Rated insulated tools protect against accidental contact.

prompt: First aid for electrical shock victim includes:

options.text: De-energize if safe, call EMS, begin CPR if needed
Pull victim by still-energized wire always always
Ignore until lunch always always
Pour water on panel always always

explanation: Ensure rescuer safety; CPR/AED if not breathing.

prompt: Confined space with electrical equipment may have atmospheric hazards including:

options.text: Only bright lighting always always
Low oxygen, toxic gases, or flammable vapors
Excess happiness always always
No hazards ever always in vaults always always

explanation: Manholes/vaults require gas testing before entry.

prompt: Live-dead-live test sequence verifies:

options.text: Only once touching bare hand always always
Meter never calibrated always always
Tester works on known live, confirms dead, then recheck live
Voltage presence ignored always always

explanation: Three-point test confirms meter function and zero energy.

prompt: GFCI devices protect personnel by:

options.text: Increasing voltage always always
Removing ground rod always always
Only protecting equipment not people always always exclusively always
Detecting imbalance between hot and neutral (~5mA trip)

explanation: GFCI trips on ground fault current through person/equipment.

prompt: Approach boundary 'restricted approach' means:

options.text: Only qualified persons with appropriate PPE may enter
Anyone may enter freely always always
No PPE needed always always
Only applies to plumbing always always

explanation: 70E boundaries define shock/arc flash limits.

prompt: Temporary wiring on construction sites must:

options.text: Use frayed cords always always
Meet NEC Article 590 including protection from damage
Run through puddles always always unprotected always always
Ignore GFCI always always

explanation: 590 permits temporary power with specific requirements.

prompt: Ladder safety near electrical equipment requires:

options.text: Metal ladder touching overhead lines always always
Only one foot on ladder always always as rule always always
Non-conductive side rails or maintain clearance from energized parts
Paint ladder with conductive foil always always

explanation: Fiberglass ladders reduce shock risk; maintain clearances.

prompt: Incident energy on 480V switchgear can be:

options.text: Always harmless always always at any fault always always
Only visible as spark never burns always always
Eliminated by wearing regular cotton always always alone always always
Severe enough to require arc-rated suit and face shield

explanation: Arc flash can cause fatal burns; PPE rated to cal/cm².

prompt: Electrical burns at entry/exit points indicate:

options.text: Current passed through body causing tissue damage
Only sunburn always always
No internal injury ever always always
Allergic reaction always always exclusively always

explanation: Internal damage may exceed visible skin burns.

prompt: Housekeeping in electrical rooms:

options.text: Store cardboard against transformer always always
Keep combustibles out of working space and equipment area
Block disconnect access always always
Oil soaked rags near breakers always always

explanation: 110.26(B) prohibits storage in required working space.

prompt: Testing for absence of voltage uses:

options.text: Guess by sound always always
Wet finger test always always
Properly rated test instrument contacting all conductors
Flip breaker randomly always always without verification always always

explanation: 110.13/70E require verified de-energization.

prompt: Qualified person per NEC means:

options.text: Any homeowner always always without training ever always always
Only engineer with PE always always exclusively always for all tasks always always
Person who owns multimeter always always regardless of knowledge always always
One who has skills and knowledge related to construction and operation for safety

explanation: Qualified = trained/experienced for equipment and hazards.
